fault_hypothesis: Lines 74-75 contain a spurious post-processing step that removes the last element whenever both inputs end with the same value, incorrectly dropping a valid duplicate timestamp from the merged result.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition on line 74 should never be true for a correct merge; when it fires it incorrectly pops a valid element.
After the pop branch executes, the merged list must still contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements.
Before returning, merged must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
The returned merged list must have length equal to sum of both input lengths.
